ai_overview: The team's primary focus has shifted toward the World Cup Predictor Game, with coordinated efforts across UX, backend infrastructure, and product planning to ensure the feature is tournament-ready. Technical stability remains a priority, evidenced by the successful movement of Cloudflare Bot Management and a critical genesis-sdk fix into the QA and review stages. You should monitor the Origins API integration closely, as developers are currently addressing feedback that may require your input to finalize. While Robin is significantly de-risking our global footprint with extensive automated testing for the navigation and footer, several high-impact items like mobile push notifications remain in the backlog. I suggest following up with Chloe and Kelvin to confirm that the World Cup Predictor's API requirements are fully aligned with the front-end roadmap to avoid integration bottlenecks.

people.name: Vic Dadson
people.summary: Focused on coordinating the implementation of Cloudflare Bot Management for the GDC platform. The development work (GDCU-8792, assigned to Sanja) has progressed to the QA phase for final testing — Vic appears to be managing this initiative from a PM perspective. No direct Jira tickets assigned to Vic in the 24h scan, consistent with a coordination role on this deliverable.

people.name: Jessica Dordevic
people.summary: Updated the guidelines for 6HPC banner creation and upload in Confluence. Ciara also set up a Claude project assistant trained specifically on 6HPC banners and tagged Jessica on it in #gdc-product-design-pod. Jira additionally shows GDCU-8906 (In Progress): reviewing Claude-generated tables on the NL Casino Strategy Page — active design/UX work not covered in Viktor's email.

people.name: Miguel Migneco
people.summary: Actively driving UX and UI design for the World Cup Predictor Game (GDCU-8935, In Progress). In #gdc-wcpredictor on 13 May he shared the Figma design file and committed to delivering a cleaned-up prototype for developers 'later today or tomorrow morning'. Attended alignment meeting with Mike and Gabriel on predictor mechanics, including a tiebreaker addition for the knockout leaderboard. Also weighed in on Ace dark mode design principles in #gdc-product-led-growth.

people.name: Ciara Carroll
people.summary: More active than Viktor's email suggested. Jira reveals two additional active tickets beyond the UI Site Audit (GDCU-8984, Backlog): the Operator List Redesign (GDCU-8619, In Progress) and World Cup Cosmo Spins Reskin (GDCU-8392, In Progress). In Slack, launched a Claude project assistant for 6HPC banner creation in #gdc-nonen-sync and committed design system updates in #gdc-product-led-growth (sports team logos added as components, DS branches merged). Also active in #gdc-product-led-growth on Ace dark mode UX discussion.

people.name: Josh Smith
people.summary: Managed several front-end UI updates including comparison cards and aligning the Free Slots carousel with the Simple Banner design. The image carousel shortcode task (GDCU-8923) was cancelled in Jira. ACE AI embedded betslips (GDCU-8789) remains in the backlog. Activity appears limited with most current projects in planning or backlog stages. In #gdc-product-design-pod, raised a design decision on the Carousel Banner Shortcode — proposing to reuse the Simple Banner with navigation arrows to reduce editor complexity.

people.name: Chloe Christie
people.summary: Coordinating actively across multiple workstreams. Cosmo Spins login issues (GDCU-8983) have moved to QA with Harden. Local navigation for news site articles (GDCU-8957) is now Done. Researching Netlify support for editorial projects (GDCU-8992, backlog) and planning for the World Cup Predictor game. Auditing social landing and email confirmation pages (GDCU-8987). This morning (14 May) in #gdc-product-design-pod, coordinated sprint closure with the UX team. Posted the midweek catchup agenda in #6amigos on 12 May.

people.name: Carlos Blauth
people.summary: Viktor's email reported no activity, but Jira shows Carlos is actively working across multiple streams: GDCU-8951 (In Progress — Learning Hub Phase 2 feedback), GDCU-8982 (In Progress — Mobile App Android/iOS package and OAuth URL scheme renaming), and GDCU-8980 (In Progress — tracking offer exit links in the mobile app). GDCU-8957 (Enable Local Navigation on News Site Articles) was recently completed. Multiple mobile app readiness tickets are also assigned to Carlos in the backlog.

people.name: Eoin McCarthy
people.summary: Finalized performance optimizations for Core Web Vitals and enabled revenue share operator lists for VIP users. Currently addressing feedback on the Origins API integration while preparing for World Cup Predictor development. In #gdc-wcpredictor on 11 May, raised detailed technical questions with Mike about game mechanics: golden boot player selection scope, how penalty outcomes interact with score predictions, and login persistence across devices. A productive kick-off meeting for the predictor was completed.

people.name: Harden Wallace
people.summary: Investigated and applied fixes for login issues on Cosmo Spins (GDCU-8983, now In QA). Also addressed a critical bug involving the genesis-sdk crashing under load on Laravel 11, moving that fix to the review stage. Both tasks represent significant debugging and bug resolution work yesterday. Chloe also tagged Harden in #6amigos to check on the country by link shortcode status.

people.name: Kelvin Yee
people.summary: Focused on planning the backend infrastructure for the World Cup Predictor, with GDCU-8995 (Implement Database and API) added to the backlog as a priority for the upcoming development cycle. Recently joined the #gdc-wcpredictor channel. Ticket remains in backlog stage, indicating early-cycle planning rather than active development. Follow-up recommended with Chloe to align API requirements with the front-end roadmap.

people.name: Milan Nikolic
people.summary: No activity in the last 24 hours. No Jira tickets or Slack messages found in any of the scanned channels.

people.name: Nebojsa Stanic
people.summary: Completed the implementation of the revenue share operator list for VIP users within ktag (GDCU-8847, Done). Currently focused on addressing feedback for the Origins API integration (GDCU-8924, In Progress), which remains his primary ongoing priority and is shared workstream with Eoin McCarthy.

people.name: Novica Vukobratovic
people.summary: Viktor's email reported no activity, but Jira shows two tickets: GDCU-8925 (Implement CWV changes recommended by Viktor AI — Done) and GDCU-8971 (Create How-to Videos for Cosmo Spins Reskin — In Product review). Also posted in #gdc-pod on 11 May asking about OptinMonster campaign ownership. More active than the email indicated.

people.name: Sanja Budic
people.summary: Viktor's email reported no activity, but Jira shows two tickets currently In QA: GDCU-8907 (Implement Betting Tips Hub Page) and GDCU-8792 (Implement Cloudflare Bot Management). Both tickets moving to QA indicates Sanja recently completed development work on these features and has handed them off for testing.

people.name: Robin John
people.summary: Developed comprehensive testing suites for Global Navigation and Footer components across 26 markets, establishing 171 specific automated tests for the footer to maintain consistency globally. GDCU-8913 (Mobile Nav Bar Audit) is marked Done in Jira. In #gdc-pod on 13 May, reported two bugs discovered during automation script writing: the Twitter/X icon on GDC SE footer redirecting to the homepage instead of the external URL, and Quick Links missing entirely on the GDC AU footer.

people.name: Shane McGrath
people.summary: Viktor's email reported no activity, but Slack shows Shane was active on 12–13 May. On 12 May he debugged a Cosmo Spins session issue flagged by Dean Ryan (session not being killed properly in mobile/desktop transitions), and logged GDCU-8978 (Password Reset flow bug — flow ends without redirect after reset). On 13 May flagged a broken arrow in the site search function. More active than the email captured.

people.name: Dean Ryan
people.summary: No direct activity in the last 24 hours. Referenced in #gdc-pod by Shane as having flagged the Cosmo Spins session issue — Dean reported the bug to Shane who is now investigating and debugging.

people.name: Michael Butlin
people.summary: Viktor's email reported no activity. In #core-division-leads on 12 May, Michael posted about clarifying the contents of an 'at risk' email being communicated to team members — suggesting active involvement in internal HR communications. Also referenced by Colin as needing to clarify the 'at risk' communications with his own team.

people.name: Dan Smullen
people.summary: Focused on the UK Casino vertical by leading a review and strategy discussion. Activity was limited to high-level planning and documentation for this specific market segment. No additional Jira or Slack activity found to supplement Viktor's email.

people.name: Gabriel Cornoiu
people.summary: Planning mobile push notification implementation using Bloomreach and OneSignal (in backlog). More active than the email indicated — in #gdc-wcpredictor on 13 May participated in an alignment meeting on WC Predictor mechanics: confirmed match results will be entered manually via the GDC Admin Panel, discussed leaderboard design and tiebreaker decisions for the knockout phase, and coordinated on geo approval and translation strategy. Also responded in #gdc-pod on 13 May about IUJ production permissions.

people.name: Diana Thurmann
people.summary: Finalizing content and SEO configurations for the GDC Iceland launch including crypto and bonus sections, while conducting competitor analysis for the Swedish market. Progressing research into interactive slot content and player interests on Confluence, and moving toward implementing unique sitewide descriptions for casino cards. The Iceland geo launch (gambling.com/is and gambling.com/is/en) was announced by Diana in #core-division on 16 April — current SEO refinement work builds on that milestone.

people.name: Colin Brannigan
people.summary: Viktor's email reported no activity. In #core-division-leads on 13 May, Colin was active in team management: advising leads that contractors won't be notified of what appears to be internal HR communications, directing leads to have individual conversations with their teams, posting 'emails are sent folks, battlestations ready' — indicating coordination around a significant internal communication event. Also shared a product features article for team discussion.
